title: Lead from the front.
description: I personally understand the complexities, constraints, and opportunities of the systems I lead. I don't manage from a remove.

title: Respect the decisions that got us here …
description: Established approaches exist because someone made a reasonable tradeoff with the information they had. Understanding why things are the way they are is a prerequisite to changing them.

title: … Then change them.
description: "We've always done it this way" is not a reason to keep doing it. Objectives evolve. Constraints shift. The work is knowing when the old tradeoff no longer holds.

title: Trust in my team scales with demonstrated judgment.
description: I give people as much autonomy as they've earned. The management job is calibrating how much independence each person can handle — and then staying out of the way.

title: Build the 30-page appendix so the 3-page executive summary is bulletproof.
description: I want to know every detail that could matter — not to present all of it, but so that when someone challenges a conclusion, I'm not guessing.
